description_long: This house features a long, rectangular lot with a main single-story structure at the front and a two-story section at the rear. The rooflines are primarily gable, with the front building having a prominent gable roof and the rear section a simpler, low-pitched gable. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely stucco or painted concrete, and the roof is a muted gray, possibly asphalt shingles or metal. The property is enclosed by low boundary walls, and there are green strips along the sides, suggesting landscaped garden beds or lawns. Windows are modestly sized and arranged in a practical, asymmetrical pattern, with the main entryway set back from the street and accessed via a paved path. The overall design is functional and modern, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A modern, functional house with a gable roof, light exterior walls, and a mix of single and two-story sections, set on a long lot with landscaped strips and boundary walls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry, boundary wall, and the paved path leading to the house, with the two-story section visible in the background.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable roof profile, the long boundary wall, and the transition from the single-story to the two-story section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the house, the two-story section, and the continuation of the landscaped strips along the lot.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the property, the boundary wall, and the arrangement of the two main building sections.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main door with a paved approach, no porch, and minimal decorative elements
